Details:
Removal of vinclozolin by biological treatment was found to be effective. One study reported 90 percent removal by an isolated strain of Rhodococcus sp. bacteria named T1-1. The study reported that strains V21 and V1 were able to remove 75 percent and 67 percent of the vinclozolin present, respectively, however the T1-1 strain of bacteria was selected because of its ability to also degrade 84 percent of vinclozolin’s more toxic metabolite, 3,5-dichloroaniline.
June 2009
